title: Chamaerhodos keweenawensis
taxonomy: Chamaerhodos keweenawensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to the United States, specifically in the Upper Peninsula of Michigan. The classification of Chamaerhodos keweenawensis includes being a member of the tribe Sanguiferae. Chamaerhodos keweenawensis is a type of shrub or subshrub. It is considered a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.

intro: Chamaerhodos keweenawensis, also known as Keweenaw sandwort, is a rare and endangered perennial plant species native to the Upper Peninsula of Michigan. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Chamaerhodos keweenawensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Keweenaw sandwort is used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, teas, and infusions, to support immune function and overall well-being.
